analgesia_and_sedation_management_during.title: Analgesia and Sedation Management during Prolonged Field Care
analgesia_and_sedation_management_during.source_file: Analgesia and Sedation Management during Prolonged Field Care
required_context: evacuation to higher level of care is not immediately possible
contraindications: Do not give analgesia and/or sedation if there are other priorities of care (e.g., hemorrhage control)
Do not give medications that lower blood pressure or suppress respiration if the patient is in hemorrhagic shock or respiratory distress
safety_gates: Always give more, but it is very difficult to take away
Start low, go slow

airway_management_in_prolonged_field_car.title: Airway Management in Prolonged Field Care
airway_management_in_prolonged_field_car.source_file: Airway Management in Prolonged Field Care
evacuation_triggers: when evacuation to higher level of care is not immediately possible

airway_management_of_traumatic_injuries.title: Airway Management of Traumatic Injuries
evacuation_triggers: secure and definitive airway not established
safety_gates: never perform blind nasal intubation
always verify tube placement with capnography if available

burn_care.title: Burn Care
evacuation_triggers: patients who cannot be evacuated from theater

burn_management_pfc.title: Burn Wound Management in Prolonged Field Care
safety_gates: intubate patients with burns covering >40% TBSA
avoid airway obstruction due to inhalation injury

catastrophic_non_survivable_brain_injury.title: Catastrophic Non-Survivable Brain Injury
evacuation_triggers: opportunity for these casualties to be evacuated safely and appropriately

damage_control_resuscitation.title: Damage Control Resuscitation
procedures: Resuscitative Endovascular Balloon Occlusion of the Aorta (REBOA)
red_flags: SBP below 110 mmHg for traumatic brain injury (TBI)
safety_gates: never give calcium for patients with ionized calcium greater than 1.2mmol/L

damage_control_resuscitation_pfc.title: Damage Control Resuscitation in Prolonged Field Care

infection_prevention_in_combat_related_i.title: Infection Prevention in Combat-related Injuries
safety_gates: monitor adherence to antimicrobial prophylaxis regimens

invasive_fungal_infection_in_war_wounds.title: Invasive Fungal Infection in War Wounds
invasive_fungal_infection_in_war_wounds.source_file: Invasive Fungal Infection in War Wounds
evacuation_triggers: massive packed red blood cell transfusion (≥20 units in the first 24 hours)
safety_gates: never delay surgical debridement for antifungal therapy

nursing_intervention_prolonged_field_car.title: Nursing Intervention in Prolonged Field Care
procedures: Wash and Dry Skin, Apply Lotion

pain_anxiety_delirium.title: Pain, Anxiety and Delirium
safety_gates: Contraindications To The Daily Sedation Holiday

progressive_return_to_activity_following.title: Progressive Return to Activity Following Acute Concussion/Mild Traumatic Brain Injury
progressive_return_to_activity_following.source_file: Progressive Return to Activity Following Acute Concussion mTBI Clinical Recommendation 2021
required_context: number of concussions in past 12 months
contraindications: Do not advance to Stage 6 until cleared by appropriate specialist
red_flags: loss of memory for events immediately before or after the injury
evacuation_triggers: presence within 50 meters of a blast
involvement in a vehicle blast event
safety_gates: never give NSAIDs within 48 hours after concussion
always perform MAC E 2 as close to time of injury as possible

reboa_for_hemorrhagic_shock.title: Resuscitative Endovascular Balloon Occlusion of the Aorta (REBOA) for Hemorrhagic Shock
reboa_for_hemorrhagic_shock.source_file: Resuscitative Endovascular Balloon Occlusion of the Aorta (REBOA) for Hemorrhagic Shock
procedures: Resuscitative Endovascular Balloon Occlusion of the Aorta (REBOA)
search_terms: Resuscitative Endovascular Balloon Occlusion of the Aorta

sepsis_management_pfc.title: Sepsis Management in Prolonged Field Care

tbi_neurosurgery_deployed_environment.title: Traumatic Brain Injury Management and Basic Neurosurgery in the Deployed Environment
required_context: environment where personnel, resources and follow-on care are limited
evacuation_triggers: Role 3 transfer for neurosurgical evaluation & care

traumatic_brain_injury_management_pfc.title: Traumatic Brain Injury Management in Prolonged Field Care

use_of_tbi_wb_biomarkers_after_potential.title: Use of Traumatic Brain Injury Biomarkers after a Potentially Concussive Event

procedures: evaluation of casualties with mild TBI

acute_extremity_compartment_syndrome.title: Acute Extremity Compartment Syndrome and Role of Fasciotomy in Extremity War Wounds
acute_extremity_compartment_syndrome.source_file: Acute Extremity Compartment Syndrome and Role of Fasciotomy (CS) in Extremity War Wounds
red_flags: delay in diagnosis can be lethal
safety_gates: prophylactic fasciotomy should be routine when compartment syndrome is likely

altitude_emergencies_prehospital_environ.title: Altitude Emergencies in the Prehospital Environment

amputation_evaluation_and_treatment.title: Amputation: Evaluation and Treatment
contraindications: do not amputate through the most proximal fracture if stabilization can improve functional outcome
safety_gates: never add iodine or castile soap to irrigation solution when irrigating open wounds
always allow for targeted muscle reinnervation if deemed appropriate

anesthesia_for_trauma_patients.title: Anesthesia for Trauma Patients
safety_gates: Do not delay induction of the patient in extremis in order to place the central venous access or invasive monitoring.

arachnid_snake_envenomation_mwd_cpg_c11.title: Arachnid and Snake Envenomation
arachnid_snake_envenomation_mwd_cpg_c11.source_file: Arachnid Snake Envenomation MWD CPG c11
safety_gates: antivenom must be given within 4 hours after a snake bite

aural_blast_injury_acoustic_trauma_and_h.source_file: Aural Blast Injury Acoustic Trauma and Hearing Loss
red_flags: symptoms of tinnitus, aural fullness, recruitment, difficulty localizing sounds, difficulty hearing in noise, vertigo

required_context: availability of specialty behavioral health care

blunt_abdominal_trauma_splenectomy_post_.source_file: Blunt Abdominal Trauma Splenectomy Post-splenectomy Vaccination
red_flags: continuing to consume blood products to maintain blood pressure
safety_gates: never transfer patients with ongoing intra-abdominal hemorrhage until assessed and controlled

cbrn_initial_response.source_file: Chemical Biological, Radiological Nuclear Injury Part1 Initial Response

crush_syndrome_pfc.title: Crush Syndrome - Prolonged Field Care
contraindications: hypotensive resuscitation principles according to TCCC
evacuation_triggers: if evacuation to higher level of care is not immediately possible
safety_gates: never apply hypotensive resuscitation in extremity crush injury requiring extrication

documentation_requirements_for_combat_ca.source_file: Documentation Requirements for Combat Casualty Care

procedures: drainage of a soft tissue abscess

emergent_resuscitative_thoracotomy.title: Emergent Resuscitative Thoracotomy (ERT)
primary_conditions: full arrest from a potentially reversible cause
required_context: performed at a forward military treatment facility with surgical and resuscitation capability
individuals familiar with and trained in this procedure
evacuation_triggers: penetrating truncal/extremity trauma with recent loss of vitals (less than 15 minutes)
blunt truncal/extremity trauma with witnessed arrest after arrival
safety_gates: ERT should only be performed at a forward military treatment facility

frozen_deglycerolized_red_blood_cells.title: Frozen and Deglycerolized Red Blood Cells
contraindications: whole blood is always preferable to using deglycerolized Red Blood Cells

procedures: suture of laceration of scrotum and tunica vaginalis

heat_injury_mwd_cpg_c9.title: Heat Injury in Military Working Dogs
safety_gates: never leave MWDs in closed vehicles
always monitor for signs of heat injury

high_bilateral_amputations_dismounted_co.source_file: High Bilateral Amputations Dismounted Complex Blast Injury

contraindications: use of sodium bicarbonate for hyperkalemia

hypothermia_prevention_treatment.title: Hypothermia: Prevention and Treatment
aliases: Hypothermia Prevention Monitoring and Management CPG
procedures: replace wet clothing with dry clothing
use passive and active methods of rewarming
safety_gates: never allow hypothermia to persist
always monitor temperature during rewarming

primary_conditions: undue suffering in Military Working Dogs (MWDs)
safety_gates: never use neuromuscular blocking agents as euthanasia agents

evacuation_triggers: bleeding that may occur during patient evacuation

safety_gates: identify all site(s) of hemorrhage
thorough examination of the pelvis and perineum

contraindications: never leave the casualty to get help if the casualty's condition may worsen
safety_gates: lifesaving direct patient care takes priority over calling for help
